weather.summary: Saturday: 12°C, clear sky. Sunday: 12°C, clear sky

metadata.greeting: This weekend is shaping up to be fantastic for outdoor adventures! I've found some delightful free activities in Hackney and across London, including museum trails and park explorations, perfect for keeping your little one entertained. Plus, I've spotted some exciting Easter events to book ahead, promising egg hunts and animal encounters galore.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive galleries with toys and exhibits for young children, including an under 2s sensory space.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers sensory experiences and interactive play designed for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check out the Mini Museum for under 2s.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a pond theme, designed for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and engaging outdoor play space.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk into the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Features exhibits about the history of London's Docklands, with child-friendly activities and interactive elements.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers hands-on activities and activity sheets specifically designed for different age groups. Has special red signs explaining things in an easy-to-read, child-friendly way.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: Check out the Mudlarks soft play area.

activities.title: Library Club
activities.description: Reading corner and book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a relaxed space for reading and discovering new books.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney.
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides an interactive space for imaginative play and storytelling, great for engaging young minds.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific baby and toddler sessions.

activities.title: St Patrick's Day Family Party
activities.description: Traditional Irish music, a family ceilidh and traditional Irish Dancing.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun, cultural experience with music and dancing.

transport.fromHackney: Check location for specific transport.
transport.tip: Look for similar events in the future.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: One of London's oldest public parks with playgrounds, a splash park, and open green spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of outdoor activities, including playgrounds and space to run around.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses available or walk along the canal.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy in the park.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals, gardens, and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides an opportunity to see and interact with farm animals.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and the wonders of the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, and plenty to capture a toddler's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the Dinosaurs gallery and try the earthquake simulator.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for young children to discover construction, water, light and sound through play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Don't miss the Wonderlab for hands-on experiments (ticketed).

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2RL
activities.description: Explore art, design, and fashion from around the world.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers daily free activities and events for children, plus backpacks of activities for exploring the museum.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Pick up a family activity pack at the Sackler Centre.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, featuring an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities suitable for toddlers, with plenty of space to run around and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Russell Square or King's Cross.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry when accompanying a child.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath, home to rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a free opportunity to see animals, including wallabies and lemurs.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then a bus or walk to the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Cutty Sark
activities.description: Explore the historic tea clipper and discover what life was like on board as a 19th-century sailor.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to explore a historic ship and participate in family activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Check for family trails and musical storytelling sessions.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore maritime history with child-friendly activities and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The All Hands Children's Gallery is specifically designed for kids, and there are worksheets available for them to enjoy as they explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check out the All Hands Children's Gallery and Character Encounters on Saturdays.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: A scenic view of London with greenery and plants, making it a sensory experience.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a free and scenic experience with great views and sensory elements.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Monument station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online as they are required even though it is free.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Cutty Sark
activities.description: An Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks — and keep your eyes peeled for the hidden eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: An exciting Easter-themed activity on a historic ship.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Unscramble a secret nautical code and claim a chocolatey treat.

activities.title: Easter at London Zoo
activities.description: Kids are invited to explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines the fun of an Easter egg hunt with a visit to the zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Road, then a bus to the zoo.
transport.tip: Look out for Easter craft activities.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter-themed trail in a historic palace garden.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court from Waterloo.
transport.tip: Match historical names to their ribbons to solve the puzzle.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens. An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and educational, perfect for young explorers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Help the Octonauts find the bees and build a new hive.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Fulham Palace
activities.description: A family-friendly activity set within one of London's oldest continually inhabited buildings. Taking place daily between 10.30am and 3.30pm, the self-guided trail weaves through both the historic palace interiors and the surrounding gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect self-guided activity that helps visitors of all ages explore the historic Palace.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Putney Bridge, then a short walk
transport.tip: Purchased on arrival from the museum front desk, with no advance booking required.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Ham House and Garden
activities.address: Ham Street, Ham, Richmond, Surrey, TW10 7RS
activities.description: A seasonal trail set within one of south-west London's most intact 17th-century estates. Located in Richmond, Surrey, the event runs daily from 10am to 5pm and is designed as a self-guided outdoor activity through the formal gardens and wider grounds.
activities.whyItWorks: The trail introduces children to historic themes through interactive tasks and activities along the route.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Richmond Station
transport.tip: Inspired by the British Civil Wars, the trail introduces children to historic themes through interactive tasks and activities along the route.

activities.title: Willows Activity Farm Easter Eggstravaganza
activities.description: A packed programme featuring an Easter Egg Hunt, Easter Grotto, arts & crafts, Peter Rabbit shows, and funfair rides.
activities.whyItWorks: Family fun including Easter Egg Hunt, Easter Grotto, Easter arts and crafts and meeting cute animals including Easter bunnies. Plus Peter Rabbit shows and Jemima Puddle Duck activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to St Albans City Station
transport.tip: Remember to book your tickets in advance!

activities.title: Spring into Wild Fangs at Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London, SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Workshops on the life cycles of various creatures, featuring the Wild Fangs team showcasing animals and their development. Attendees can engage with some animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Ideal opportunity for young nature-lovers and budding evironmentalists to have fun with interactive nature-inspired games

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill Station
transport.tip: Don't forget to book tickets in advance!
